item.title: sanitizer uses process.env.HOME instead of os.homedir()
item.intent: Audit log and quarantine paths are always computed correctly — never fallback to /root. os.homedir() is the single correct API for user home directory.
item.description: src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts:24-26 computes DEFAULT_OPS_DIR using process.env.HOME ?? '/root' instead of os.homedir() from the 'os' module. The os module is the correct cross-platform API — process.env.HOME is undefined in some container and test environments. If HOME is unset, audit logs and quarantined files are written to /root, which is an incorrect path and may be unwritable. Fix: import { homedir } from 'os'; replace the HOME env var reference with homedir(). The correct pattern is already used in src/config/paths.ts. Steps: (1) open src/secure/sanitizer/sanitizer.ts; (2) add homedir import from 'os'; (3) replace process.env.HOME ?? '/root' with homedir(); (4) run existing sanitizer tests to confirm no regression. Scope: one-line change in one file.
